name: Cleopatra Beach
description: A scenic beach featuring a natural rock pool (legendary “Cleopatra’s Bath”) with clear turquoise water where visitors float in a sheltered saltwater pool .

name: Agiba Beach
description: A picturesque cove famous as “The Wonder Beach,” nestled between white limestone cliffs with panoramic views of turquoise waters . The beach’s name (Ajiba meaning “wonder”) reflects its natural beauty .

name: Al-Gharam Beach
description: A long sandy beach known from classic Egyptian cinema. It’s famous for being the spot where actress Leila Mourad sang in the 1950s film Shati al-Gharam , making it a nostalgic “Lover’s Beach” (Beach of Love).

name: El-Awam Beach
description: A city beach near the Sidi El-Awwam mosque

name: El-Obayed Beach
description: A wide sandy beach in western Matrouh

name: El-Ramla Beach
description: A sandy city beach with gentle surf

name: Cleopatra Rock
description: A prominent natural rock formation on Cleopatra Beach. It has a tunnel-like opening (“Cleopatra’s Bath”) that fills with water at high tide, forming a natural pool said to be used by Queen Cleopatra .

name: Rommel Cave Museum
description: A World War II museum housed in the sea caves that were Field Marshal Erwin Rommel’s headquarters in 1942. It displays Rommel’s personal items and military artifacts . The museum (opened in 1977) was established in honor of Rommel and reopened in 2017 after restoration.

name: Salt Cave Salman
description: An indoor salt therapy attraction modeled after traditional salt caves. Visitors can relax in a lounge area surrounded by salt-coated walls. It’s a modern wellness spot (salt from Siwa Oasis) promoted for respiratory relaxation.

name: Souq Libya (Libyan Market)
description: The traditional market of Marsa Matruh, famous for spices, dates, olive products, and handmade goods. It is a “lively market” noted for affordable local goods and herbal products . Vendors sell souvenirs, textiles, and local produce.

name: Shali Fortress
description: 13th-century mudbrick fortress (karshef) on a rocky outcrop, once home to the town. Largely ruined by 1926 rains .

name: Temple of the Oracle of Amun
description: Ancient 26th-Dynasty Temple (oracle of Zeus-Amun) at Aghurmi (~4 km NE of Siwa) where Alexander the Great was confirmed as pharaoh .

name: Mountain of the Dead – Gebel al-Mawta
description: Limestone necropolis hill with ~3,000 rock-cut tombs from 26th Dynasty through Roman/Byzantine times. It’s a major archaeological site (tombs of Si-Amun, Mesu-Isis, “Crocodile” tomb, etc.).

name: Fatnas Island
description: Small palm-covered island on Siwa’s main salt lake (Birket Siwa), reachable by causeway. Renowned for spectacular sunsets and a simple lakeside café .
